subject: Re: MTools: importing population from Windows WARP
body:
Hi Jesse,

Apologies for the lack of communication here, I've been teaching a workshop
and traveling.

As you've noted, things have changed quite a bit between 1.10 and 2.0.
Major version changes like this generally mean that things are not expected
to 'just work' when upgrading.

A migration tool might be possible but as of right now is not planned - the
recommendation would be to start from scratch in Warp 2.0.
I understand that this can be frustrating if you already have particle
picks. It should be possible to reuse your tilt-series alignment results
and thus your picks but will take a little bit of work and you will have to
check things carefully.

I've opened an issue for the possible future migration tool at
https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/188

Good luck!

Alister

On Tue, 23 Jul 2024 at 11:44, Jesse Hansen <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:

> Looking closely at the settings files from windows WARP to Linux WARP it
> seems this is not going to be easy.  There are many differences between
> them including the XML encoding, some parameter names (e.g. Folder vs
> DataFolder), the Linux version has the XML split into two files (frame
> series vs tilt series), and other differences.
>
> My coding is not advanced enough to convert from the windows XML to the
> Linux XML formatting.
>
> best,
>
> Jesse
>
> On Monday, July 22, 2024 at 10:01:33 AM UTC+2 Jesse Hansen wrote:
>
>> hi all,
>>
>> no update yet, wondering if anyone out there has successfully processed
>> earlier steps with windows WARP then switched over to Linux Warp ?
>>
>> best
>>
>> Jesse
>>
>> On Friday, July 19, 2024 at 12:35:16 PM UTC+2 Jesse Hansen wrote:
>>
>>> hi all,
>>>
>>> I have processed my data already in windows WARP, but now I'd like to
>>> pick up on Linux M.  Is that possible, or do I need to start over
>>> processing fresh in WarpTools?
>>>
>>> I have gone through WarpTools tutorial so I know all functionality is
>>> good.
>>>
>>> For my data, I cannot create the data source where I point to my WARP
>>> previous.settings file.  When it runs I get the output below:
>>>
>>> jhansen@gpu144$ ./datasource.sh
>>> Running command create_source with:
>>> population = m/mydata.population
>>> processing_settings = previous.settings
>>> name = mydata
>>> nframes =
>>> files =
>>>
>>> Looking for data... Unhandled exception. System.ArgumentException: The
>>> value cannot be an empty string. (Parameter 'path')
>>>    at System.ArgumentException.ThrowNullOrEmptyException(String
>>> argument, String paramName)
>>>    at System.IO.Path.GetFullPath(String path)
>>>    at System.IO.Enumeration.FileSystemEnumerator`1..ctor(String
>>> directory, Boolean isNormalized, EnumerationOptions options)
>>>    at System.IO.Enumeration.FileSystemEnumerable`1..ctor(String
>>> directory, FindTransform transform, EnumerationOptions options, Boolean
>>> isNormalized)
>>>    at System.IO.Enumeration.FileSystemEnumerableFactory.UserFiles(String
>>> directory, String expression, EnumerationOptions options)
>>>    at System.IO.Directory.InternalEnumeratePaths(String path, String
>>> searchPattern, SearchTarget searchTarget, EnumerationOptions options)
>>>    at MTools.Commands.CreateSource.Run(Object options) in
>>> (path)/warp/MTools/Commands/CreateSource.cs:line 108
>>>    at MTools.MTools.Run(Object options) in
>>> (path)/warp/MTools/MTools.cs:line 32
>>>    at CommandLine.ParserResultExtensions.WithParsed[T](ParserResult`1
>>> result, Action`1 action)
>>>    at MTools.MTools.Main(String[] args) in
>>> (path)/warp/MTools/MTools.cs:line 21
>>> ./02.sh: line 4: 492165 Aborted                 MTools create_source
>>> --name mydata --population m/mydata.population --processing_settings
>>> previous.settings
>>>
>>> any help is appreciated,
>>>
>>> best,
>>>
>>> Jesse
>>>
>>> --
